texto: [The waves] move across a faint horizon, the rush of love and the surge of grief, the respite of peace and then fear again, the heart that beats and then lies still, the rise and fall and rise and fall of all of it, the incoming and the outgoing, the infinite procession of life. And the ocean wraps the earth, a reminder. The mysteries come forward in waves.
explicacao: Citação de Susan Casey sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “[The waves] move across a faint horizon, the rush of love and the surge of grief, the respite of …” articula amor, vida, ocean em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e ocean.
